title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn (Gwenith) and Jiles visit Doctor Kennedy to discuss Gwendolyn's past. Gwendolyn reveals she has purchased a mountain-side villa in Dealmore, which Kennedy identifies as St. Catherine's Manor, the house where Gwendolyn previously lived. Gwendolyn explains her motive for returning is a sense that the place feels like home, despite her hazy memories.

Gwendolyn seeks information regarding her father and her sister, Helen. Kennedy recounts that Helen returned from India on the same ship as Gwendolyn's father, Kelvin Harley. Although Kelvin was a single father at the time, they married in London and lived in Deal. However, less than a year later, Helen ran away with another man, possibly a married Roman Catholic. Kennedy notes that Kelvin did not want a divorce. Regarding Gwendolyn's father, Kennedy suggests he sent her to New Zealand due to pressure from her mother's relatives following the breakdown of his second marriage.

When Gwendolyn asks about her father's death, Kennedy claims his health was poor and he died in a nursing home on the East Coast two years after leaving Dilworth. Kennedy avoids providing the specific location of the nursing home or the site of the grave, dismissively arguing that focusing on the past is a mistake. Kennedy further reveals that he was Helen's half-brother and had helped her through past troubles before she married Walter Fane in India. Kennedy expresses a desire to contact Helen, but Gwendolyn and Jiles state they do not know her whereabouts. Gwendraith concludes that they hoped to contact Helen to learn more about her father.
